title: Leaderboards
seoTitle: Game of Darts Leaderboards | BarkinMad Studios
description: Review Game Center leaderboard support for X01, Cricket, Snooker, Bob's 27, 1 to 20, and Halve It in Game of Darts.
summary: Leaderboards require Game Center sign-in and are displayed through the app's Leaderboards screen.
heroImageAlt: Artwork for Game of Darts leaderboards image

sections.heading: Supported surfaces
paragraphs: The Leaderboards screen displays Match Darts highest score plus selectable X01 variants, Cricket highest score, Snooker variants, Bob's 27 best score, 1 to 20 least darts, and Halve It highest score.
Game Center sign-in is required for leaderboards.

sections.heading: Submission rules
paragraphs: Leaderboards track formats such as X01 highest score, best checkout, best average, most 180s, and 1 to 20 least darts.
You can control leaderboard auto-posting in the app settings.

images.alt: Artwork for Game of Darts leaderboards
images.caption: Leaderboard screenshot

featureShowcases.heading: Compare scores through Game Center
featureShowcases.summary: Leaderboards help players compare supported results after matches, from X01 scores to selected mode-specific challenges.
body: This matters because many players want a target after the match ends. Leaderboards make strong scores and personal bests part of the ongoing Game of Darts loop.
Players use this screen after signing in to Game Center, completing supported games, and posting eligible results.
bullets: Supports Game Center comparison for selected modes and scoring categories.
Gives competitive players a reason to revisit formats.
Pairs with statistics and achievements after play.

image.alt: Game of Darts leaderboard screen screenshot
image.caption: The leaderboard screenshot shows the competitive follow-up after supported matches.

featureShowcases.heading: Control how scores are posted
featureShowcases.summary: Set score sharing in settings, then compare your eligible results on leaderboards.
body: This matters because players should understand when their scores are shared through Game Center. Settings keep leaderboard behaviour transparent and adjustable.
Players use settings to control supported posting behaviour, then return to leaderboards to compare eligible results.
bullets: Keeps comparison separate from local score entry.

image.caption: Leaderboard artwork supports Game Center comparison guidance.
